title: Winter Walk
content:
The holly bush, a sober lump of green,
    Shines through the leafless shrubs all brown and grey,
    And smiles at winter be it eer so keen
    With all the leafy luxury of May.
    And O it is delicious, when the day
    In winter's loaded garment keenly blows
    And turns her back on sudden falling snows,
    To go where gravel pathways creep between
    Arches of evergreen that scarce let through
    A single feather of the driving storm;
    And in the bitterest day that ever blew
    The walk will find some places still and warm
    Where dead leaves rustle sweet and give alarm
    To little birds that flirt and start away.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Clare, titled "Winter Walk", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
